protocol_text:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Age >= 18
- Diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes

Exclusion Criteria:
- eGFR < 30 mL/min

cases.description: Clear positive case: adult, correct diagnosis, healthy kidney function.
Fails diagnosis inclusion criterion (no Type 2 Diabetes).

patient.notes: Borderline renal function, just above exclusion threshold.

cases.description: Edge case: eGFR just above the exclusion cutoff.
Edge case: exactly at the minimum age boundary.

patient.notes: No recent renal panel on file.

cases.description: Missing eGFR lab value: exclusion criterion can't be confidently assessed, so nothing excludes the patient, but the verdict should be flagged for review rather than trusted outright.

patient.notes: Diagnosis history not available in this record.

cases.description: Missing diagnosis data: inclusion criterion can't be confirmed, so it is conservatively treated as not met and flagged for review.

patient.notes: Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes since childhood.

cases.description: Known mock-LLM limitation: naive keyword matching cannot distinguish Type 1 from Type 2 diabetes, so it confidently mismatches this patient as eligible. A real LLM call would reason over the distinction; this case documents where the deterministic mock's simplicity breaks down.
Compound boundary case: near-minimum age and eGFR just below the exclusion cutoff at the same time.
